input.new_string:
#!/bin/sh
input=$(cat)

used=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
used_tokens=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.current_usage.input_tokens // empty')
total=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.context_window_size // empty')

if [ -n "$used" ] && [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  printf "\033[36mCtx:\033[0m %.0f%% used, %.0f%% remaining" "$used" "$remaining"
  if [ -n "$used_tokens" ] && [ -n "$total" ]; then
    printf " (%s / %s tokens)" "$used_tokens" "$total"
  fi
else
  printf "\033[36mCtx:\033[0m no data yet"
fi

The file /root/.claude/settings.json has been updated successfully.

content.text:
The status line is now configured. Here is a summary of what was set up:

**Files modified/created:**

- `/root/.claude/statusline-command.sh` - The status line script
- `/root/.claude/settings.json` - Updated with the `statusLine` command entry

**What the status line displays:**

When context data is available (after the first API call in a session), it shows:

```
Ctx: 42% used, 58% remaining (8400 / 200000 tokens)
```

When no messages have been sent yet:

```
Ctx: no data yet
```

The label "Ctx:" is rendered in cyan using ANSI colors, which will appear appropriately dimmed in the terminal status line area.

---

Note: For any further changes to the status line — such as adding git branch info, model name, rate limit usage, or any other fields — please use the **statusline-setup** agent again. You can ask Claude to make adjustments at any time and it will update `/root/.claude/statusline-command.sh` and `/root/.claude/settings.json` accordingly.

input.old_string:
#!/bin/sh
input=$(cat)

used=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
used_tokens=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.current_usage.input_tokens // empty')
total=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.context_window_size // empty')

if [ -n "$used" ] && [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  printf "\033[36mCtx:\033[0m %.0f%% used, %.0f%% remaining" "$used" "$remaining"
  if [ -n "$used_tokens" ] && [ -n "$total" ]; then
    printf " (%s / %s tokens)" "$used_tokens" "$total"
  fi
else
  printf "\033[36mCtx:\033[0m no data yet"
fi
input.new_string:
#!/bin/sh
input=$(cat)

remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
total=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.context_window_size // empty')
used_tokens=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.current_usage.input_tokens // empty')

if [ -n "$remaining" ] && [ -n "$total" ] && [ -n "$used_tokens" ]; then
  remaining_tokens=$(echo "$total $used_tokens" | awk '{printf "%d", $1 - $2}')
  printf "\033[36mCtx left:\033[0m %.0f%% (%s tokens)" "$remaining" "$remaining_tokens"
elif [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  printf "\033[36mCtx left:\033[0m %.0f%%" "$remaining"
else
  printf "\033[36mCtx left:\033[0m no data yet"
fi

content.content: The file /root/.claude/statusline-command.sh has been updated successfully.

content.text:
The status line script at `/root/.claude/statusline-command.sh` has been updated. It now shows only how much context is left to use, in this format:

- When full data is available: `Ctx left: 85% (170000 tokens)`
- When only the percentage is available: `Ctx left: 85%`
- When no data yet: `Ctx left: no data yet`

The remaining token count is calculated by subtracting the input tokens used from the total context window size.

Note: For any further status line changes, the "statusline-setup" agent should be used — and you can ask Claude at any time to continue adjusting the status line to your liking.
